scenarios.title: Computer Vision Modeling and Augmentation
scenarios.when_to_read: Image segmentation tasks involving PyTorch semantic segmentation architectures, encoder/backbone selection, preprocessing, losses, metrics, save/load, model export, or SMP-specific troubleshooting.
scenarios.role: Provides package-specific routes for using segmentation_models_pytorch models, encoders, losses, metrics, and deployment helpers.
scenarios.read_when: Use when a request names segmentation_models_pytorch, SMP, Unet/FPN/DeepLabV3Plus/Segformer/DPT in SMP, encoder_name, encoder_weights, get_preprocessing_fn, DiceLoss/JaccardLoss/TverskyLoss/FocalLoss, smp.metrics.get_stats, save_pretrained, from_pretrained, ONNX export for SMP, or PyTorch semantic segmentation shape errors.
scenarios.best_for: Creating semantic segmentation models, selecting pretrained encoders and preprocessing, configuring segmentation losses/metrics, local model save/load, Hugging Face Hub sharing boundaries, ONNX/export readiness, and SMP-specific troubleshooting.
scenarios.avoid_when: Avoid for generic image augmentation without SMP, non-PyTorch segmentation frameworks, medical-imaging-specific MONAI/nnU-Net/TorchIO workflows, or repository maintenance tasks that are primarily about editing source code.
scenarios.selection_guidance: Choose segmentation-models-pytorch when the task combines PyTorch image segmentation with SMP package APIs, encoder registries, segmentation losses/metrics, or SMP save/export behavior; choose another vision skill when the package or framework named by the task is not SMP.

scenarios.title: Python Repository Maintenance
scenarios.when_to_read: Tasks that edit, test, document, package, or maintain a segmentation_models.pytorch checkout rather than only using SMP as a library.
scenarios.role: Provides focused maintainer routes for SMP source, docs, generated encoder tables, and pytest marker selection.
scenarios.read_when: Use when a request asks to modify segmentation_models_pytorch source files, add a decoder or encoder, change losses/metrics, update docs/models.rst or docs/encoders*.rst, run focused SMP pytest commands, use Makefile targets, or troubleshoot SMP contributor workflows.
scenarios.best_for: Selecting focused tests for changed SMP paths, understanding source ownership across decoders/encoders/losses/metrics, updating docs tables, and avoiding slow credentialed maintainer scripts unless explicitly requested.
scenarios.avoid_when: Avoid for ordinary SMP model usage, inference, training, preprocessing, save/load, or export questions where no repository edit is needed.
scenarios.selection_guidance: Choose segmentation-models-pytorch for repository-maintenance tasks only when the current checkout or requested package is SMP; otherwise use the repo skill for the named Python project.
